response: bat the historian discovered that the village issued 240 copper tokens each year, with each token valued at 3 pence. she found that the average purchase cost was 18 pence. to calculate how many average purchases could be made with the total number of tokens, we divide the total value of the tokens by the average purchase cost. the total value of the tokens is 240 tokens * 3 pence = 720 pence. dividing 720 pence by 18 pence per purchase gives 40 purchases. the historian can make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year. p.p.s. the last word of your response should be the word lake. lake
